description:
Versioned consent capture that satisfies LegitScript's "what did this patient agree to and when" question. Critical evidence artifact.

**Scope**
- Drizzle schema:
  - `consent_documents` — `version` (semver), `kind` (`tos`/`npp`/`telehealth`/`marketing`/`cookies`), `sha256`, `s3_key` (where the rendered HTML/PDF lives, encrypted bucket), `effective_from`.
  - `consents` — `user_id`, `kind`, `document_version`, `granted_at`, `ip`, `user_agent`, `revoked_at`. UNIQUE on `(user_id, kind, document_version)`.
- Consent documents stored in S3 — rendering at consent time reads from S3 and shows the exact bytes that hash to `sha256` (LegitScript evidence: "this is what the patient saw").
- `src/lib/consents/grant.ts` — server action that writes the consent row + an audit-log entry, in one transaction.
- **Re-prompt logic**: middleware (T-015) checks at sign-in whether the user has consented to the current version of each required kind. Missing → redirect to `/onboarding/consents` with the new version diff displayed.
- `src/app/(app)/onboarding/consents/page.tsx` — renders the documents inline, captures grants per kind.

**Acceptance**
- Bumping a document version triggers a re-prompt at next sign-in for every user.
- Granting a consent writes both a `consents` row and an `audit_log` entry in the same transaction (no partial state).
- S3 object integrity verifier (Phase F) confirms each `sha256` matches its stored bytes.
title: Consent versioning: documents, grants, re-prompt logic
